mechanism_narrative: A positive 3-month trend in FEDFUNDS raises the discount rate applied to equity cash flows and tightens financial conditions, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if periods with positive 3-month FEDFUNDS changes produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of walk-forward triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is above 4.5%, the high policy-rate level keeps equity discount rates elevated and compresses valuation multiples, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if FEDFUNDS-above-4.5% observations have average 20-day S&P 500 returns greater than the unconditional average by at least 25 bps.

mechanism_narrative: If CPIAUCSL rises over 3 months while FEDFUNDS remains above 4.5%, markets price persistent inflation and restrictive policy together, reducing expected equity multiples and generating negative 60-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition has a nonnegative mean 60-day S&P 500 return or if its hit rate for negative returns is below 50% in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE above 4.0% signals weakening labor demand and rising recession risk, lowering expected corporate earnings and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE crosses above 4.0% and rises over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations show positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or a negative-return hit rate statistically indistinguishable from baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Positive 3-month growth in both RSAFS and PAYEMS indicates household income and spending strength, supporting revenue expectations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint positive-growth condition fails to outperform unconditional 60-day S&P 500 returns by at least 50 bps on average in walk-forward evaluation.

mechanism_narrative: A positive 6-month trend in INDPRO signals improving production volumes and operating leverage, raising expected earnings and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive 6-month INDPRO trend observations have average 60-day S&P 500 returns below the unconditional average or Rank IC below 0.03 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in HOUST below 1300 signals weakening interest-sensitive demand and a softer construction pipeline, reducing cyclical earnings expectations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST-below-1300 episodes produce mean 60-day S&P 500 returns above zero and above the unconditional mean in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS growth remains positive while UNRATE is below 4.5%, labor income growth dominates recession risk, supporting consumption and causing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint labor-resilience condition has a negative mean 5-day S&P 500 return or underperforms unconditional 5-day returns in more than 55% of triggered observations.
